item.title: 我在 AWS re:Invent 2022 上作为 Dev Chat 演讲者的经历 | My Experience as a Dev Chat speaker at AWS re:Invent 2022
item.description:
在上一届 AWS reInvent 2022 上，我第一次能够在社区轨道下的 Dev Chat 中发言。 我谈到了我的一个项目——使用 AWS Serverless 的智能门铃。 这篇文章解释了我作为第一次 Dev Chats 演讲者的经历。

这篇文章专门针对我自己的经验，您和/或其他演讲者的经验可能会有所不同。

我有机会成为 AWS 社区建设者计划的成员。 请在此处阅读有关该计划的更多信息： aws.amazon.com/developer/community/comm ...

重新发明之前

应用

之后，准备工作开始了！

幻灯片平台准备

这包括字体、字体大小、颜色和任何图像特定准则。 由于 reInvent 中的所有会议都是公开的，因此幻灯片中的所有材料都将由 AWS 活动文案编辑团队进行验证。

要求尽快准备好幻灯片并提交审核。 提交后，文案编辑组将对幻灯片进行审核，如需修改，需重新调整后再提交审核。

幻灯片组获得批准后，即为将要呈现的版本，并且不允许进行更多修改。 但是，如果需要任何关键更新，您可以在 reInvent 园区的演讲者就绪室会议上提出要求。

参加网络研讨会前须知

为了提供有关我们作为演讲者在 reInvent 上可以期待什么的信息，AWS 安排了几个网络研讨会。 这些被称为“出发前了解”网络研讨会，由 AWS 全球活动团队主持。

这些网络研讨会对我来说真的很有用，因为这是我第一次参加 reInvent。

口语辅导课

在此过程中，我获得的另一个绝佳机会是可以请一位演讲教练来排练我的演讲。 可以在线预订以及在 reInvent 校园内预订有限的口语辅导课程。 我预订了一个 60 分钟的在线课程。

我与 AWS 的高级全球公共演讲教练Blaine Sundrud进行了这次会议。 这是我第一次与演讲教练互动，我对他提供的意见感到非常惊讶。 首先，我向他展示了我的演讲，他为我提供了一些关于如何改进演讲的重要提示。 此外，他甚至建议对幻灯片进行一些修改，以使观众更清楚地了解整个故事。 此外，由于我在演讲中准备了一个演示，他提供了一些改善用户交互的重要提示。

如果您要出席 AWS 活动并且有演讲者辅导选项，我强烈建议您利用这个机会，因为这肯定会以积极的方式改变您在公共场合的展示方式！

在重塑园区

扬声器准备室

这应该在谈话前至少 4 小时完成。 这样，活动经理就会知道演讲者出现在 reInvent 校园中。 建议为 SRR 预订一个时间段，即使也有很少的预约。

首先，演讲者可以浏览他们的演示幻灯片，并在演示前使用 Copy Editor 最后一次验证内容。 也可以在复制编辑器的帮助下对幻灯片进行一些小的修改。

接下来，您可以在同一个 SRR 中进行“技术检查”。 在这里，您可以检查观众如何看到您的幻灯片。 此外，您将快速了解声音设备、指点设备的工作原理，如何在幻灯片和您的计算机或任何其他设备（如果适用）之间切换等。此外，如果您需要 wifi 连接到您的附加设备，可以安排在 SRR 中。

对于那些喜欢内部演讲者培训的人，也可以在 SRR 进行预订。

在 SRR 上完成所有事情后，您就可以开始演讲了。

图片：扬声器准备室

谈话

我的演讲安排在周三下午 4 点，我必须提前半小时到达世博区的 Dev Lounge。 这是为了确保一切设置正确。 这包括连接我自己的笔记本电脑、与 Raspberry PI 设备的互联网连接（用于演示）以及对视听单元的快速测试。

由于人很多，所以这个地方总是有点吵。 因此，与会者获得了耳机来收听演讲。

我的演讲总共有 30 分钟，其中包括演示以及一些问题和答案。 就我个人而言，我很高兴地展示了我的项目并得到了很好的反馈。 非常感谢决定出席我的演讲的与会者。

重塑之后

回馈

在 reInvent 之后不久，下周一我收到了基于与会者调查的关于我的会议的反馈。 这非常快，对我作为演讲者非常有帮助。

概括

我有机会加入AWS Community Builders 计划。 因此，我要感谢 AWS 社区建设者项目经理 Jason Dunn ( @jasondunn ) 和团队（抱歉，人数太多无法在此一一提及）给我机会，这绝对有助于我继续我的工作！

总的来说，我觉得整个过程组织得很好，非常注重细节。 尽管我是 reInvent 的第一次演讲者，但我对整个过程感到很自在，因为与我互动的所有人都给予了我所有的支持。 非常感谢参与组织这次亚马逊活动的所有人。

感谢 AWS 提供的出色体验。

图片：Jason Dunn 和一些 reInvent 2022 Dev Chats 演讲者



原文:

At the last AWS reInvent 2022, I was able to speak in Dev Chat under the Community track for the first time. I talked about one of my projects - a Smart doorbell with AWS Serverless. This post explains my experience as a first time Dev Chats speaker.

This post is on specifically my own experience and yours and/or other speakers' experience may vary.

I got this opportunity being a member of the AWS Community Builders program. Please read more about the program here: aws.amazon.com/developer/community/comm…

Before the reInvent

Application

In the month of June 2022, Jason Dunn, the AWS Community Builders Program Manager has sent a message to AWS Community Builders' Slack, stating that the CFP is open for the talks under community track at reInvent 2022. I decided to submit an application for my project - Smart doorbell with AWS Serverless for a Dev Chat which is a 30 minute talk.

In mid August, I received an email from Shantavia Craigg, AWS Developer Event Program Manager with the confirmation of my Dev Chat session approval.

After that, the preparations started!

Slides deck preparation

AWS has provided a PowerPoint theme, styles and guideline as to how to prepare the slides. This includes the font, font size, colors and any image specific guidelines. Since all the sessions in reInvent are public, all the material that goes into the slides will be verified by the AWS events copy editing team.

It was required to prepare the slides deck as soon as possible and submit it for review. Once submitted, the copy editing team will review the slides and if any modifications are required, those need to be adjusted and submitted again for review.

Once the slides deck is approved, that will be the version going to be presented and no more modifications are allowed. However, if there is any critical update required, you can request them at the Speaker Ready Room session at the reInvent campus.

Know Before You Go Webinar

In order to provide information on what we can expect at reInvent as a speaker, there were a couple of webinars arranged by AWS. Those were named as Know Before You Go webinars, which were conducted by AWS Global Events Team.

These webinars were really useful for me, as this was my first time at reInvent.

Speaking Coaching Session

Another great opportunity I had in this process is to have access to a speaking coach to rehearse my talk. There were limited speaking coaching sessions available to book online as well as within reInvent campus. I booked an online session which was for 60 minutes.

I had this session with Blaine Sundrud, Senior Global Public Speaking Coach at AWS. This was my first experience interacting with a speaking coach and I was really amazed by the input he has given. First, I presented my talk to him and he provided me with some great tips on how to improve the talk. Also, he even suggested some modifications to the slides in order to make the whole story much clearer to the audience. Also, since there was a demo I prepared in the talk, he had some great tips to improve user interaction.

If you are to present at an AWS event and there is a speaker coaching option, I highly recommend using that opportunity because that will definitely change how you present in public in a positive way!

At the reInvent Campus

Speaker Ready Room

Every speaker of reInvent should check-in to a Speaker Ready Room (SRR). This should be done at least 4 hours before the talk. This way, event managers will get to know that the speaker is present in the reInvent campus. It is recommended to book a time slot for SRR even though few walk-ins are also available.

There are few things that happen in the SRR. First, speakers can go through their presentation slides deck and verify the contents for one last time before the presentation with a Copy Editor. It is possible to do some minor modifications to the slides with the help of the Copy Editor too.

Next, you can do a "Tech Check" in the same SRR. Here, you can check how your slides are visible to the audience. Also, you will be given a quick walk through on how the sound devices, pointing devices work, how switching between slide deck and your computer or any other devices (if applicable) can be done etc. Also, if you need wifi connections to your additional devices, that can be arranged in SRR.

For those who prefer in-house speaker training, this is also available with a reservation at the SRR.

Once everything is finalised at the SRR, you are ready for the talk.

Image: Speaker Ready Room

The Talk

My talk was scheduled on Wednesday at 4 PM and I had to be at the Dev Lounge in the Expo area half an hour before. This was to make sure everything was set up correctly. This included connecting my own laptop, internet connection to raspberry PI device (for the demo) and a quick test on audio visual units.

Dev Lounge is in the Expo area which is an open space. Since there are a lot of people, the place is always a bit noisy. Therefore the attendees were given headphones to listen into the talks.

I had a total of 30 minutes for my talk which includes the demo and a few questions and answers as well. Personally I had a great time presenting my project and got nice feedback as well. Thanks a lot to the attendees who decided to be there for my talk.

After the reInvent

Feedback

Soon after the reInvent, next Monday I received the feedback about my sessions based on the attendees' survey. Which was quite fast and very helpful for me as a speaker.

Summary

I got this opportunity to be a part of the AWS Community Builders program. So, I would like to thank AWS Community Builders Program Manager Jason Dunn (@jasondunn), and the team (sorry, there's too many people to mention here individually) for the opportunity which definitely helps me to continue what I do!

Overall, I feel the whole process was well organised with highly attention to details. Even though I am a first time speaker at reInvent, I was comfortable going through this whole process because of all the support I was given from all the people I interacted with. Thanks a lot everyone involved in organising this amazon event.

Thank you AWS for the great experience.

Image: Jason Dunn and some of the reInvent 2022 Dev Chats speakers
item.author: pubudusj

item.title: 打字稿：函数 | Typescript: Functions
item.description:
在本文中，我们将学习如何在函数中使用 TypeScript。 以及如何限制函数采用与定义参数不同类型的值。 这将是对函数的基本介绍，因为我们将深入本系列，您将学到更多关于函数的知识，例如如何使用类型别名或接口为函数定义自定义类型。

这将是一个完整的打字稿系列，您将从字符串、布尔值等基本主题到类型别名、枚举、接口、泛型等更复杂的主题学习。

目录

职能

当你了解 JS 时，编写函数是小菜一碟，但在打字稿中它会变得有点复杂。 别担心，我们会处理好所有方面的问题。 我们需要在函数参数和返回类型中定义两个事物的类型。

参数

函数参数是函数定义中列出的名称。 我将举一个我之前提到过的旧例子：

 // This is a norma JS Function that take two number and returns the sum // Problem is when you call the function you can pass any value // It won't show error because the variable does not have any kind of type function increaseScore(currentScore, increaseBy){ return currentScore + increaseBy; } // Now we define that both parameters have `number` type and it will only take the number // otherwise it will throw an error function increaseScore(currentScore: number, increaseBy: number) { return currentScore + increaseBy; }

以下是将错误值传递给函数时将显示的错误示例：

 function increaseScore(currentScore:number, increaseBy:number){ console.log(currentScore + increaseBy); } increaseScore(10, 2) // ✅ Correct increaseScore(10, "2"); // ❌ Error increaseScore(10, [2,3,4]) // ❌ Error

返回类型

返回类型很重要。 因为在打字稿中有很多返回类型。 例如，您已经知道boolean 、 number和string 。 但这里的问题是我们如何定义函数返回的类型。 您可以通过以下语法来做到这一点。

 // Syntax function funcName(para: paraType): returnType { //........ } // For Example: function greetings(name: string): string { return "hello" + name; } greetings("john"); // ✅ greetings(true); // ❌ ERROR: Expected String // greet is 'string' type because greetings() return stirng type let greet = greetings("Don"); greet = 2; // ❌ ERROR: because type is 'string'

其他类型

空白

只要函数没有任何return语句或不从这些返回语句返回任何显式值，它就是推断类型。

 // This function doesn't return anything thus its return type is void function sayHi(name: string): void { console.log("Hi! " + name); }

绝不

在返回类型中，这意味着函数抛出异常或终止程序的执行。

 function handleError(errMsg: string): never { throw new Error(errMsg); }

还有更多其他类型，您可以查看文档以进一步使用。

可选参数

当你定义参数时，有时你不需要传递参数。 因此，为此，您可以添加? 在参数旁边，如以下代码所示：

 function doSomething(num?: number) { // ... } doSomething(); // ✅ OK doSomething(10); // ✅ OK

使用对象

在打字稿中使用对象可能会让你觉得有点奇怪。 为什么？ 您将在本节末尾知道原因。 在许多情况下您都可以使用对象。 让我们一一看看——

传递对象作为参数

将对象作为参数传递可能有点棘手。 您需要定义要传递的每个属性的类型，如以下代码所示：

 // Destructuring an Object function signUp({email, password}: {email: string, password: string}): void{ console.log(email); } // You can also define the signUp function like the following function signUp(user: {email: string, password: string}): void{ console.log(user.email); } signUp(); // ❌ ERROR: need to pass an object signUp({}); // ❌ ERROR: to pass an object with email & password , signUp({email: "hello@gmail.com", password: "12345678"}); // ✅ Correct

现在，如果你想传递一个不止这两个参数的对象怎么办：

 function signUp(user: { email: string; password: string }): void { console.log(user); } // Passing name in the signUp function // ❌ ERROR: 'name' does not exist signUp({ email: "hello@j471n.in", password: "12345678", name: "Johnny" }); // Creating a separate object and then passing it with the name // ✅ Correct and No Error, But if you use 'name' in the signUp function then you'll get an error let newUser = { email: "hello@j471n.in", password: "12345678", name: "Johnny" }; signUp(newUser);

从函数返回对象

您可以通过多种方式从函数返回对象，以下代码中显示了其中一些方式以及它是否正确。

 // ❌ ERROR: A function whose declared type is neither 'void' nor 'any' must return a value // As function needs to return an object with name & age properties function getInfo():{name: string, age: number}{} // ❌ ERROR: Property 'age' is missing // Function must have all the properties as specified (name, age) // And It only returns the name that's why it throws an error function getInfo():{name: string, age: number}{ return {name: "John"}; } // ✅ CORRECT // No Error Because all the things are correct function getInfo():{name: string, age: number}{ return {name: "John", age: 29}; } // ❌ ERROR: 'lastName' does not exist // As function should return only 'name' and 'age' // And it returns 'lastName' too function getInfo():{name: string, age: number}{ return {name: "John", age: 29, lastName: "Doe"}; } // ✅ CORRECT // You can assign an object to some variable and then return it // Even if it has more properties as described function getInfo():{name: string, age: number}{ let user = {name: "John", age: 29, lastName: "Doe"} return user; } // ❌ ERROR: A function whose declared type is neither 'void' nor 'any' must return a value // As you can see it has two {} // First {} shows that it should return an object // Second {} is function definition // It should return an object function getInfo():{}{} // ✅ CORRECT // It returns and object that's why it works, It can have any properties because we haven't specified function getInfo():{}{ return {name: "John"} }

上面的代码示例可能看起来很吓人，但我们也可以使用类型别名来实现这些目的。 我们将在下一篇文章中介绍。

包起来

在这篇文章中，我解释了如何在函数中使用 typescript。 以及如何限制函数采用与定义参数不同类型的值。 这将是对函数的基本介绍，因为我们将深入本系列，您将学到更多关于函数的知识，例如如何使用类型别名或接口为函数定义自定义类型。

这是一系列 Typescript，可帮助您从头开始学习 Typescript。 如果您喜欢这篇文章，请不要忘记给❤️和书签🏷️供以后使用，如果您有任何问题或反馈，请不要犹豫，将它们放在下面的评论中。 我会在下一篇中看到。

与我联系

推特领英网站通讯支持


原文:

In this article, we are going learn about How you can use typescript in functions. And How to restrict the function to take different types of values than define parameters. It will be a basic introduction to functions as we go in-depth in this series you will learn so much more about functions such as How you can use Type Aliases or Interface to define custom types for Functions.

This is going to be a full series of typescript where you will learn from basic topics like string, boolean to more complex like Type Aliases, enums, Interface, generics, and etc.

Table of Contents

Functions

Writing function is a piece of cake when you know the JS but it gets a little bit complex in typescript. Don’t worry, we will take every aspect of that. We need to define the types of two things in function Parameters & Return Types.

Parameters

Function parameters are the names listed in the function's definition. I'll take an old example that I've mentioned before:

// This is a norma JS Function that take two number and returns the sum 
// Problem is when you call the function you can pass any value 
// It won't show error because the variable does not have any kind of type
function increaseScore(currentScore, increaseBy){ return currentScore + increaseBy;
} // Now we define that both parameters have `number` type and it will only take the number
// otherwise it will throw an error
function increaseScore(currentScore: number, increaseBy: number) { return currentScore + increaseBy;
}

Following is an example of the error it will show when you pass the wrong value to the function:

function increaseScore(currentScore:number, increaseBy:number){ console.log(currentScore + increaseBy);
} increaseScore(10, 2) // ✅ Correct
increaseScore(10, "2"); // ❌ Error
increaseScore(10, [2,3,4]) // ❌ Error

Return Types

Return types matter. Because In typescript there are many return types. For example, you already know boolean, number and string. But the question here is how we defined which type should return from the function. You can do that by the following syntax.

// Syntax
function funcName(para: paraType): returnType { //........
} // For Example:
function greetings(name: string): string { return "hello" + name;
} greetings("john"); // ✅
greetings(true); // ❌ ERROR: Expected String // greet is 'string' type because greetings() return stirng type
let greet = greetings("Don");
greet = 2; // ❌ ERROR: because type is 'string'

Other Types

void

void represents the return value of functions that don’t return a value. It’s the inferred type any time a function doesn’t have any return statements or doesn’t return any explicit value from those return statements.

// This function doesn't return anything thus its return type is void
function sayHi(name: string): void { console.log("Hi! " + name);
}

never

The never type represents values that are never observed. In a return type, this means that the function throws an exception or terminates the execution of the program.

function handleError(errMsg: string): never { throw new Error(errMsg);
}

There are a lot more other types you can take a look at the documentation for further use.

Optional Parameters

When you define parameters, sometimes you don't need to pass the parameters. So for that, you can add ? next to the parameter as shown in the following code:

function doSomething(num?: number) { // ...
}
doSomething(); // ✅ OK
doSomething(10); // ✅ OK

Working with Object

In typescript working with objects could make you feel a little weird. Why? You will know why at the end of this section. There are many instances where you can use objects. Let's look at them one by one-

Passing Object as Parameter

Passing an object as a Parameter could be a little tricky. You need to define the types of each property you are passing as shown in the following code:

// Destructuring an Object
function signUp({email, password}: {email: string, password: string}): void{ console.log(email);
} // You can also define the signUp function like the following
function signUp(user: {email: string, password: string}): void{ console.log(user.email); } signUp(); // ❌ ERROR: need to pass an object
signUp({}); // ❌ ERROR: to pass an object with email & password ,
signUp({email: "hello@gmail.com", password: "12345678"}); // ✅ Correct

Now, what if you want to pass an object with more than these two parameters:

function signUp(user: { email: string; password: string }): void { console.log(user);
} // Passing name in the signUp function
// ❌ ERROR: 'name' does not exist
signUp({ email: "hello@j471n.in", password: "12345678", name: "Johnny" }); // Creating a separate object and then passing it with the name
// ✅ Correct and No Error, But if you use 'name' in the signUp function then you'll get an error
let newUser = { email: "hello@j471n.in", password: "12345678", name: "Johnny" };
signUp(newUser);

Returning Object from Function

You can return an object through many ways from a function some of them are shown in the following code along with whether is it correct or not.

// ❌ ERROR: A function whose declared type is neither 'void' nor 'any' must return a value
// As function needs to return an object with name & age properties
function getInfo():{name: string, age: number}{} // ❌ ERROR: Property 'age' is missing
// Function must have all the properties as specified (name, age)
// And It only returns the name that's why it throws an error
function getInfo():{name: string, age: number}{ return {name: "John"};
} // ✅ CORRECT 
// No Error Because all the things are correct
function getInfo():{name: string, age: number}{ return {name: "John", age: 29};
} // ❌ ERROR: 'lastName' does not exist 
// As function should return only 'name' and 'age'
// And it returns 'lastName' too
function getInfo():{name: string, age: number}{ return {name: "John", age: 29, lastName: "Doe"};
} // ✅ CORRECT 
// You can assign an object to some variable and then return it 
// Even if it has more properties as described
function getInfo():{name: string, age: number}{ let user = {name: "John", age: 29, lastName: "Doe"} return user;
} // ❌ ERROR: A function whose declared type is neither 'void' nor 'any' must return a value
// As you can see it has two {}
// First {} shows that it should return an object
// Second {} is function definition
// It should return an object
function getInfo():{}{} // ✅ CORRECT 
// It returns and object that's why it works, It can have any properties because we haven't specified
function getInfo():{}{ return {name: "John"}
}

The above code example might be scary to look at but we can achieve these things with Type Aliases as well. We'll look at it in the next article.

Wrapping up

In this article, I explained How you can use typescript in functions. And How to restrict the function to take different types of values than define parameters. It will be a basic introduction to functions as we go in-depth in this series you will learn so much more about functions such as How you can use Type Aliases or Interface to define custom types for Functions.

This is a series of Typescript that will help you to learn Typescript from the scratch. If you enjoyed this article, then don't forget to give ❤️ and Bookmark 🏷️for later use and if you have any questions or feedback then don't hesitate to drop them in the comments below. I'll see in the next one.

Connect with me

Twitter GitHub LinkedIn Instagram Website Newsletter Support
item.author: j471n

item.title: 使用哪个：Typescript 中的接口或类型别名？ | Use which: Interface or Type Alias in Typescript?
item.description:
本博客主要讨论interface vs type的主要区别，并分享我将如何在日常软件开发程序中使用它们。

描述对象的形状或函数签名

两者都可用于描述对象的形状或函数签名。

但是，我大多用interface来描述对象，用type来描述一个函数签名，因为我可以清楚地知道它是arrow function对函数的定义，而这在interface中是不可能实现的。

 interface Point { x: number; y: number; } type SetPoint = (x: number, y: number) => void;

我使用接口的另一个场景是「索引签名」。 例如，在动态生成的输入中，我们无法提前知道类型属性的所有名称，但我们知道值的形状。

 interface Input { [index: string]: string | number; }

扩展和交叉类型

乍一看，扩展和交集是组合类型的类似方式。 然而，两者之间的主要区别在于如何处理冲突。

对于interface ，如果您使用相同的键声明一个属性，它将覆盖派生接口中的属性。 但是，它应该与前一个兼容，否则 typescript 编译器会抛出错误。

 // This works pretty well, as it is compatible interface Converter { convert: (value: number) => string; } interface StringNumberConverter extends Converter { convert: (value: number | string) => string; } // Oops...this will throw an error, as it is incompatible interface Converter { convert: (value: number) => string; } interface StringNumberConverter extends Converter { convert: (value: string) => string; }

对于类型，具有相同键的属性不必兼容。 你可以声明任何你喜欢的。

 type Converter = { convert: (value: number) => string; } type StringNumberConverter = Converter & { convert: (value: string) => string; }

您应该注意的另一个区别是它们所代表的含义。 当我使用接口时，我希望它代表「父子」关系。 相比之下，当我使用类型时，它只代表「组合」关系。

 // 「Parent-Child」relationship. Cat derives from Animal. interface Animal { name: string; } interface Cat extends Animal { color: string; } // 「Composition」relationship. Color and Size are two seperate species type Color = { name: string } type Size = { size: 'small' | 'medium' | 'large' } type ColorSize = Color & Size

类实现

一个类可以实现接口或类型别名，但不能实现联合类型，就像在打字稿中一样，一个类只能实现一个对象类型或对象类型与静态已知成员的交集。

所以在日常开发中，如果这个类型是设计成类实现的，我会使用interface over type 。

 type PartialPoint = { x: number; } | { y: number; }; // can not implement a union type class SomePartialPoint implements PartialPoint { x = 1; y = 2; }

不仅仅是对象类型

与接口不同，类型别名还可以用于其他类型。 经常使用的是联合类型和元组。

 type StringNumberPair = [string, number]; function doSomething(stringHash: [string, number]) { const [inputString, hash] = stringHash; console.log(inputString); // string console.log(hash); // number }

声明合并

一个接口可以被定义多次，并且将被视为一个接口，所有声明的成员都被合并。

 type Bear = { name: string; } // Error：Duplicate Identifier type Bear = { age: number; } // works pretty well interface Bear { name: string; } interface Bear { age: number; } const b: Bear = { name: '', age: 0 }

使用typeof类型运算符

在大多数情况下，如果您想使用typeof类型运算符，请选择type over interface 。

 function f() { return { x: 10, y: 3 }; } type P = ReturnType<typeof f>; /** ** type P = { x: number; y: number; } **/

结论

下次当您对使用哪个感到困惑时，试试这个备忘单！

永恒的开发帖子...

我多年前就希望知道的 Git 概念

开发人员使用最多的技术不是 Javascript。

它不是 Python 或 HTML。

它甚至很少在面试中被提及或被列为工作的先决条件。

当然，我说的是 Git 和版本控制。

一旦暂停，magentaqin 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

解除暂停后，magentaqin 将可以再次发表评论和发帖。

一旦取消发布，magentaqin 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 magentaqin 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您保护 DEV 社区 👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 您可以通过以下方式标记洋红色：

降低 magentaqin 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

不屈不挠的 magentaqin 将恢复他们帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

This blog mainly discusses the key differences of interface vs type, and share how I would use them in my daily software development routine.

Describe the shape of an object or a function signature

Both can be used to describe the shape of an object or a function signature.

However, I mostly use interface to describe the object, and use type to describe a function signature, as I can clearly know it’s a definition for function from arrow function, which is impossible to realize in interface.

interface Point { x: number; y: number;
} type SetPoint = (x: number, y: number) => void;

Another scenario where I use interface is for 「Index Signatures」. For example, in dynamic generated input, we don’t know all the names of a type’s properties ahead of time, but we do know the shape of the values.

interface Input { [index: string]: string | number;
}

Extending and Intersection Types

At first sight, extending and intersection are similar ways to combine types. However, the principle difference between the two is how conflicts are handled.

For interface, If you declare a property with the same key, it will overrides the one in the derived interface. However, it should be compatible with the previous one, or typescript compiler will throw an error.

// This works pretty well, as it is compatible
interface Converter { convert: (value: number) => string;
} interface StringNumberConverter extends Converter { convert: (value: number | string) => string;
} // Oops...this will throw an error, as it is incompatible
interface Converter { convert: (value: number) => string;
} interface StringNumberConverter extends Converter { convert: (value: string) => string;
}

For type, properties with the same key don’t have to be compatible. You can declare whatever you like.

type Converter = { convert: (value: number) => string;
} type StringNumberConverter = Converter & { convert: (value: string) => string;
}

Another difference you should pay attention is the meaning they represent. When I use interface, I want it stands for the「Parent-Child」relationship. By contrast, when I use type, it only stands for 「Composition」relationship.

// 「Parent-Child」relationship. Cat derives from Animal.
interface Animal { name: string;
} interface Cat extends Animal { color: string;
} // 「Composition」relationship. Color and Size are two seperate species
type Color = { name: string
}
type Size = { size: 'small' | 'medium' | 'large'
} type ColorSize = Color & Size

Class Implements

A class can implement an interface or type alias, but it can not implement Union type, as in typescript, a class can only implement an object type or intersection of object types with statically known members.

So in daily development, if this type is designed to be implemented by class, I will use interface over type.

type PartialPoint = { x: number; } | { y: number; }; // can not implement a union type
class SomePartialPoint implements PartialPoint { x = 1; y = 2;
}

More than Object Types

Unlike an interface, type alias can also be used for other types. Frequently used are such as union type and tuples.

type StringNumberPair = [string, number];
function doSomething(stringHash: [string, number]) { const [inputString, hash] = stringHash; console.log(inputString); // string console.log(hash); // number 
}

Declaration Merging

interface is open, and type alias is close within itself. An interface can be defined multiple times, and will be treated as a single interface with members of all declarations being merged.

type Bear = { name: string;
}
// Error：Duplicate Identifier
type Bear = { age: number;
} // works pretty well
interface Bear { name: string;
} interface Bear { age: number;
} const b: Bear = { name: '', age: 0
}

Working With typeof Type Operator

In most cases, if you want to work with typeof type operator, choose type over interface.

function f() { return { x: 10, y: 3 };
} type P = ReturnType<typeof f>; /**
** type P = { x: number; y: number;
}
**/

Conclusion

When the next time you are confused which to use, try this cheatsheet!

Timeless DEV post...

Git Concepts I Wish I Knew Years Ago

The most used technology by developers is not Javascript.

It's not Python or HTML.

It hardly even gets mentioned in interviews or listed as a pre-requisite for jobs.

I'm talking about Git and version control of course.

Francisco Maria Calisto - Jan 3

Once suspended, magentaqin will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, magentaqin will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by magentaqin will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If magentaqin is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Note:

Thanks for keeping DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻 safe. Here is what you can do to flag magentaqin:

Make all posts by magentaqin less visible

magentaqin consistently posts content that violates DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging magentaqin will restore default visibility to their posts.
item.author: magentaqin

item.title: 反思 2022 年：博客统计数据回顾 | Reflecting on 2022: A Review of the Blog's Statistics
item.description:
这是反思 2022 年博客的最佳时机。 在这篇博文中，我将分享这些数字。 哪些页面最受欢迎？ 是什么推动了最多的流量，以及在此过程中的一些意想不到的学习。

浏览量

对我来说最令人兴奋的指标是页面浏览量。 我的读者喜欢什么内容？ 比去年的1588次浏览量增加了118.51%。

电影回顾展仍然排名第一。但我怀疑关于工程经理薪水的页面很快就会超过它。 前 5 名中的两个帖子是 2022 年写的帖子！ 这让我对未来充满希望。

绝对最差的页面？ 我仍然觉得每月摘要是个好主意。 但我认为执行时事通讯会更有益。 需要考虑的事情。

流量来源

是什么为博客带来了最多的访问量？


热门流量来源

我在学习 SEO 相关技能方面的努力开始得到回报。 自然流量从 35.5% 增长到50.9% ！ 特别感谢Tech with Maddy 的 Maddy 。 谁给了我一些关于如何在不花钱购买昂贵工具的情况下进行 SEO 研究的重要提示。 谢谢，麦迪！

分解社交网站的流量告诉我，Twitter 和 Linkedin 是主要的驱动因素。 这是我通常在相关领域/小组中分享我的博客文章的地方。

推荐流量主要来自dev.to我分享博客的地方。 当人们点击链接到我的博客时。 有163 位访客从那里经过。

学识

在流量增长的同时，我也从意想不到的来源获得了流量。 我了解到Google Lens是一回事，我显然从中获得了少量流量。 一些竞争对手（我假设）通过 Ahrefs、Engagementbuilder 或其他 Seo 工具访问博客。

我尝试了不同的 SEO 方法。 并找到了一个适合我的。 我没有看到任何巨额回报利基网站或 SEO 大师的承诺。 但这对我来说都是微不足道的收获。

每个用户访问的页面数目前为1.3 。 在这一年中，添加了几项内容以使用户更容易阅读更多内容。 侧边栏现在有 2 个帖子块，每个帖子都有来自同一类别的帖子滑块。 我很好奇明年该指标是否会有所改善。

我不喜欢新年决心。 你可以在任何一天开始改变。 您不必等待新年（或星期一）。 所以我不打算在这里做出任何决定。 但我想改进的是我的写作习惯。 我想养成一个真正的习惯。 我目前在一周内突发性地写 3 篇博客文章，然后一个月什么都不写。 我希望它能以更稳定的速度变化。

关于写作的话题。 我想尝试更多的写作形式。 我的博客文章通常采用随笔风格。 虽然我想分享自发的想法，或者我注意到的事情。 这可能提供的信息较少，但更发人深省。

我还想在 Linkedin 上分享更多内容。 我注意到我几乎没有在那里分享我所有的帖子，只有我认为会做得很好的帖子。 当我在 Linkedin 上分享时，这通常会带来流量激增。 我觉得与在 Twitter 上分享的对比是匿名的。 老实说，我有时有点害怕负面评论。

你走之前

首先，感谢您阅读我对 2022 年博客的评论。一如既往地写作是一种乐趣，看到博客的成长和人们喜欢我创建的内容让我感到温暖。 希望2023年和2022年一样成功，下次审核的目标是10.000 page views。

如果您喜欢这种风格的帖子并希望更新更多此类帖子？ 订阅时事通讯！



原文:

2022 has come to an end. This is the perfect time to reflect on 2022 for the blog. In this blog post, I will share the numbers. What pages were the most popular? What drove the most traffic, and some unexpected learnings along the way.

Pageviews

The most exciting metric for me is page views. What content did my readers like? In 2022 the blog managed to get a whopping 3470 pageviews. A 118.51% increase over last year's 1588 views.

The top 5 pages of 2022 where:

The top 5 pages of 2022

The movie retrospective is still going strong as number 1. But I suspect the page about engineering managers' salaries will surpass it soon. Two posts in the top 5 are posts that were written in 2022! That makes me hopeful for the future.

The absolute worst page? The monthly digest of April 2021 got 0 views. I still feel the monthly digest was a good idea. But I think the execution would be more beneficial as a newsletter. Something to think about.

Traffic sources

What drove the most traffic to the blog?


Top Traffic sources

My efforts in learning SEO-related skills are starting to pay off. Organic traffic grew from 35.5% to 50.9%! A special thanks to Maddy from Tech with Maddy. Who gave me some great tips on how to do SEO research without breaking the bank on expensive tools. Thanks, Maddy!

Breaking down the traffic from Socials tells me that Twitter & Linkedin are the main drivers. This is where I usually share my blog posts in relevant niches/groups.

The traffic on referrals is primarily from dev.to where I share my blogs. And when people click on links to my blog there. A nice 163 visitors came through there.

Learnings

While traffic grew, I also got traffic from unexpected sources. I learned that Google Lens is a thing, and I apparently get a small amount of traffic from it. Some competitors (I assume), visit the blog through Ahrefs, Engagementbuilder, or other Seo tools.

I tried different approaches to SEO. And found one that works for me. I don't see any of the mega returns niche sites or SEO guru's promise. But it is all about tiny gains for me.

The number of pages visited per user is currently 1.3. During the year several things were added to make it easier for users to read more. The sidebar now has 2 blocks of posts, and each post has a slider of posts from the same category. I'm curious to see if that metric will improve in the next year.

2023

I don't like new years resolutions. You can start to change any day. You don't have to wait for the new year (or a Monday). So I'm not going to make any resolutions here. But what I do like to improve is my writing habit. I'd like to create a real habit. I currently write in bursts of writing 3 blog posts in a week, and then nothing for a month. I'd like that to change to a more steady pace.

On the topic of writing. I would like to experiment more with the form of my writing. My blog posts are most often essay styled. While I would like to share spontaneous ideas, or things that I notice. Which might be less informative, and more thought-provoking.

I also would like to share more content on Linkedin. I notice I do not nearly share all my posts there, and only the ones I think will do well. And when I share on Linkedin this usually brings a spike in traffic. I feel the contrast with sharing on Twitter is the anonymity. And to be honest I'm sometimes a bit scared of negative comments.

Before you go

First of all, thank you for reading my review of the blogs 2022. As always it has been a pleasure to write, and it warms my heart to see the blog grow and people like the content I create. I hope that 2023 will be as successful as 2022, and I will aim for 10.000 page views in the next review.

If you liked this style of post and want to be updated with more of these posts? Subscribe to the newsletter!
item.author: smeetsmeister

item.title: API：Explicando el Overfetching y Underfetching de manera amigable。 | APIs: Explicando el Overfetching y Underfetching de manera amigable.
item.description:
佛朗哥·戈麦斯

发表于 1 月 4 日

问题💥

市长使用 de recursos。

  • 弹珠🔍

    想象一下 estas en un restaurante punto de hacer tu orden。 请求：

     GET /api/pizza?flav=pepperoni HTTP/1.1

    过度获取 ➕

    不足 ➖

    维莫斯：

     { "name": "Pepperoni", "size": "slice", },

    可能的解决方案🤔

    我们正在寻找一名高级全栈工程师加入开发团队。

    前往此处了解更多信息。

    • 帕维尔·拉扎列夫 - 22 年 12 月 27 日

    一旦暂停，gomezfrannco 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

    解除暂停后，gomezfrannco 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

    一旦取消发布，gomezfrannco 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

    如果 gomezfrannco 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

    笔记：

    感谢您保护 DEV 社区 👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记 gomezfrannco 的方法：

    降低 gomezfrannco 的所有帖子的可见度

    报告其他不当行为

    取消标记 gomezfrannco 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



    原文:

    Franco Gomez

    Posted on Jan 4

    El overfetching y el underfetching son dos problemas comúnmente considerados "errores" en el diseño de una API que impactan directamente en el funcionamiento de ésta.
    En éste post veremos las desventajas que traen estos conceptos y sus posibles soluciones a la hora de diseñar nuestras APIs.

    El problema 💥

    El overfetching es producido cuando el recurso entregado contiene más información de la que inicialmente fue solicitada. Esto supone un problema ya que es innecesariamente costoso para el servidor.

    Por otra parte, el underfetching es todo lo contrario. Cada vez que se solicita información, la API devuelve menos datos de los que el cliente pide, es decir, proporciona información incompleta. El resultado de esto es una frustración por parte del cliente y un mayor número de peticiones para obtener el recurso completo.

    Algunas desventajas ❌

    • Aumento en el número de peticiones.
    • Mayor uso de recursos.
    • Más complejidad en el código.
    • Exposición de datos.

    Ejemplo 🔍

    Imagina que estas en un restaurante a punto de hacer tu orden. Le pides al mesero una pizza de pepperoni 🍕 y esperas pacientemente a que traigan tu orden.
    Esto que te acabo de decir, representa una petición al servidor en donde el cliente **es la persona que solicita la pizza de pepperoni, la **API es el mesero y el **servidor **es el restaurante o la cocina en su defecto.

    La petición:

    GET /api/pizza?flav=pepperoni HTTP/1.1

    La respuesta que esperamos:

    { "name": "Pepperoni", "size": "medium", "ingredients": [ "tomato sauce", "mozzarella cheese", "pepperoni", ],
}

    Veamos como el overfetching y el underfetching dificultan la usabilidad de la API.

    Overfetching ➕

    En el caso del overfetching, luego de que el cliente haya esperado pacientemente por su pizza de pepperoni, el mesero llega con la orden. Al ver la orden el cliente nota algo un tanto diferente en su tan ansiada pizza de pepperoni. Veamos que pasa:

    [ { "name": "Pepperoni", "size": "medium", "ingredients": [ "tomato sauce", "mozzarella cheese", "pepperoni", ], }, { "name": "Meat Lovers", "size": "large", "ingredients": [ "tomato sauce", "mozzarella cheese", "pepperoni", "ham", "bacon", "sausage" ] }, { "name": "Hawaiian", "size": "medium", "ingredients": [ "tomato sauce", "mozzarella cheese", "ham", "pineapple" ] }, { "name": "Vegetarian", "size": "small", "ingredients": [ "tomato sauce", "mozzarella cheese", "mushrooms", "bell peppers", "onions" ] },
]

    ¿Qué ha pasado? La pizza de pepperoni llegó acompañada de otras 3 pizzas. El cliente solo necesita la información sobre la pizza de pepperoni, pero el mesero ha traído toda la información disponible sobre todas las pizzas. Esto puede resultar muy costoso ya que puede requerir más tiempo para procesar y transmitir la información innecesaria, lo que puede aumentar el tiempo de respuesta de la API y disminuir la velocidad de la aplicación.

    Underfetching ➖

    Para el underfetching, el cliente ha esperado por su orden pero cuando ve llegar al mesero, nota que solo trae una porción de la pizza. Veamos:

    { "name": "Pepperoni", "size": "slice",
},

    Esta porción ni siquiera tiene ingredientes! El cliente pidió una pizza de pepperoni pero el mesero solo ha traído una porción sin ingredientes. Esto logra frustrar al cliente ya que solicitó un recurso pero éste fue entregado de manera incompleta, ahora deberá seguir realizando peticiones. Esta dificultad a la hora de usar nuestra API puede provocar una mayor cantidad de solicitudes para obtener toda la información necesaria, lo que puede aumentar la carga en el servidor y disminuir la eficiencia del sistema.

    Posibles soluciones 🤔

    Para mitigar estos problemas aquí tienes algunos consejos que puedes seguir a la hora de diseñar una API:

    1. Documenta tu API: Puedes minimizar el underfetching y el underfetching ayudando a los desarrolladores a entender como funciona tu API documentándola de forma correcta.
    2. Mejorar la arquitectura de la API: Puedes intentar diseñar las rutas y el acceso a los recursos de una mejor manera siguiendo buenas prácticas.
    3. DTOs: Utiliza DTOs (Data Transfer Objects) para controlar qué información es enviada y recibida.
    4. GraphQL: Puedes utilizar GraphQL para que tus clientes puedan solicitar solo la información necesaria.

    Muchas gracias por llegar hasta aquí, espero que hayas comprendido lo que he escrito en este post.

    Sígueme si te ha sido de ayuda! 😸

    We're looking for a Senior Full Stack Engineer to join the DEV team.

    Head here to learn more.

    Pavel Lazarev - Dec 27 '22

    ดูซีรีย์เกาหลี Reborn Rich ตอนที่ 16 ซับไทย THAI-DUB (FINALE) - Dec 24 '22

    Francisco Mendes - Dec 27 '22

item.title: 使用 React App 创建生日提醒 | Create a Birthday Reminder with React App
item.description:
奥斯汀陈

发表于 1 月 4 日

介绍：

  • 学习从静态 JS 文件加载数据
  • 学习使用 useState 来存储数据
  • 学习清除状态变量并查看 UI 中的反映

应用程序组件 h3 应包含以下文本：

今天有 0 个生日

根据 data.length 设置今天有多少人生日

当将数据作为 props 传递给 List 组件时，我们可以解构 props 并映射传递的数据。

对于 App.js 组件中的清除所有按钮如下：

  • 创建一个名为 people 的新状态变量，并使用 State Hook 接受初始值作为从顶部导入的数据。
  • 在 List 组件中传递此状态变量。
  • 当按下清除按钮时，清空状态变量，以便不显示任何记录（提示：将其设置为空数组）

结语搭建生日提醒react app，可以帮助新手理解基本的useState Hook，导出数据然后存入App.js变量。

在我的 github 页面中查看更多详细信息： github.com/chenyibo406


我还更新了 netify 中的生日应用程序，请参阅我的项目： birthday-reminder-ac.netlify.app/

这篇帖子在 2020 年的 DEV 上火了：

作为 JavaScript 开发者，我们通常不必自己处理编译器。 然而，了解 JavaScript 引擎的基础知识并了解它如何处理我们对人类友好的 JS 代码并将其转化为机器可以理解的东西绝对是一件好事！ 编码愉快！

一旦被暂停，chenyibo406 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，chenyibo406 将可以重新发表评论和发帖。

一旦取消发布，chenyibo406 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 chenyibo406 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您保护 DEV 社区 👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记 chenyibo406 的方法：

降低 chenyibo406 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 chenyibo406 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Austin Chen

Posted on Jan 4

Introduction:

  • learn to load data from a static JS file
  • learn to use useState to store data
  • learn to clear the state variable and see that reflected in the UI

1. Create the HTML hierarchy in App.js:

  • App component h3 should contain the following text:

0 birthdays today

2. Create the List component in App.js and pass the data as props to List component:

set how many people birthdays today based on the data.length

3. The data file have all the image url, id, age, and name for List component:

4. List component

When pass the data as props to List component, we can deconstruct the props and map the passing data.

5. Add a “Clear” button which will reset it to 0 birthdays.

For the clear all button as the following in App.js component:

  • Create a new state variable called people and useState Hook to accept the initial value as the imported data from the top.
  • Pass this state variable in the List component.
  • When the Clear button is pressed, empty the state variable so that no record is displayed (Hint: set it to an empty array)

Conclusion Building the birthday reminder react app can help newbie understanding the basic useState Hook, export data and then store in the App.js variable.

see more detail in my github page: github.com/chenyibo406


I also update the birthday-app in netify, see my project at: birthday-reminder-ac.netlify.app/

This post blew up on DEV in 2020:

🚀⚙️ JavaScript Visualized: the JavaScript Engine

As JavaScript devs, we usually don't have to deal with compilers ourselves. However, it's definitely good to know the basics of the JavaScript engine and see how it handles our human-friendly JS code, and turns it into something machines understand! 🥳

Happy coding!

Helitha Rupasinghe - Jan 1

item.title: Laravel 开发简介：特性、优点和入门 | An Introduction to Laravel Development: Features, Benefits, and Getting Started
item.description:
它由 Taylor Otwell 于 2011 年创建，此后成为最受欢迎的 PHP 框架之一，拥有庞大而活跃的开发人员社区。 观看2023 年您应该使用哪个 PHP 框架？ 在YouTube上。

在本文中，我们将探索 Laravel 开发的主要特性和优势，以及它与其他流行的 PHP 框架的比较。 我们还将深入研究 Laravel 开发的基础知识，包括安装、路由以及使用控制器和视图。

开发人员选择 Laravel 的主要原因之一是其丰富的功能和工具集，可以轻松构建现代、功能丰富的 Web 应用程序。 视图，代表用户界面； 和控制器，它处理用户输入并与模型和视图通信。 这种架构有助于确保干净、模块化的代码库，并使开发人员更容易编写和维护代码。

这使得查询和操作数据变得更加容易，并有助于减少需要编写的 SQL 代码量。

依赖注入： Laravel 使用依赖注入来管理类的依赖关系，使得代码的管理和测试更加容易。 依赖注入允许开发人员在构造函数中指定类的依赖关系，从而更容易换出不同的实现和模拟依赖关系以进行测试。

反向路由： Laravel 的反向路由功能允许开发人员使用命名路由生成 URL，从而更容易重构路由并在整个应用程序中保持一致的 URL 结构。

包生态系统： Laravel 拥有庞大而活跃的开发人员社区，他们创建了范围广泛的包和工具，可以轻松集成到 Laravel 应用程序中。 这些包可以帮助向应用程序添加新特性和功能，例如支付处理、社交媒体集成等。

其他一些流行的 PHP 框架包括：

它已经存在了很长时间，因此它具有您的项目所需的寿命和支持。 它以模块化设计和灵活性着称，经常用作大型复杂项目的基础。 它以注重安全性和性能着称，经常用于企业级项目。 它以快速的性能和高效的数据库访问而著称，经常用于大型项目。 对于需要优化其应用程序性能的开发人员来说，Yii 可能是一个不错的选择，但可能不如 Laravel 对初学者友好。

要开始使用 Laravel 开发，您需要在计算机上安装 PHP 和 Web 服务器（例如 Apache 或 Nginx）。 安装这些先决条件后，您可以使用 Laravel 安装程序工具创建一个新的 Laravel 项目。 这可以通过运行以下命令来完成：

 composer create-project --prefer-dist laravel/laravel your-project-name

这将在具有指定名称的目录中创建一个新的 Laravel 项目。 然后，您可以导航到项目目录并通过运行以下命令启动 Laravel 开发服务器：

这将启动开发服务器并使您的 Laravel 应用程序可以在localhost:8000访问。

在 Laravel 中，路由用于定义映射到应用程序不同部分的 URL。 路由可以在 routes/web.php 文件中定义，该文件位于 Laravel 项目的根目录中。

下面是一个简单路由的示例，它定义了一个 URL 和相应的控制器操作：

 Route::get('/', 'HomeController@index');

在此示例中， get方法指定应可通过 HTTP GET 请求访问此路由，并且/ URL 映射到HomeController控制器的index操作。 当用户导航到/ URL 时，将执行index操作并显示相应的视图。

例如：

 Route::get('users/{id}', 'UserController@show');

在此示例中， {id}​​ 参数将传递给UserController控制器的show操作，并可用于显示特定用户的数据。

在 Laravel 中，控制器用于处理用户输入并与应用程序的模型层和视图层进行通信。 控制器通常在app/Http/Controllers目录中定义，并且通常按它们负责的功能或资源进行组织。

下面是一个定义索引操作的简单控制器示例：

 <?php namespace App\Http\Controllers; use Illuminate\Http\Request; class HomeController extends Controller { public function index() { return view('home.index'); } }

在此示例中， index操作返回一个名为home.index的视图，它位于resources/views目录中。

视图用于定义应用程序的用户界面，通常用 HTML 编写，嵌入了用于显示数据和逻辑的 PHP 代码。 下面是一个显示用户列表的简单 Blade 模板示例：

 @extends('layout.app') @section('content') <h1>Users</h1> <ul> @foreach ($users as $user) <li>{{ $user->name }}</li> @endforeach </ul> @endsection

在这个例子中， @extends指令指定这个视图应该扩展一个名为layout.app的布局视图，它定义了页面的整体结构。 结论

其丰富的功能和工具集，包括 MVC 架构、Eloquent ORM、Artisan 控制台、Blade 模板引擎和依赖注入，使其成为构建各种 Web 应用程序的开发人员的热门选择。 无论您是初学者还是经验丰富的开发人员，Laravel 都提供了一个友好而强大的开发环境，可以帮助您快速高效地构建高质量的 Web 应用程序。

在 YouTube 上查看更多内容

观看我的视频，我会告诉您 2023 年应该使用的流行 PHP 框架的优缺点。您可以查看下面的嵌入式视频或单击文章开头的链接在 YouTube 上观看。



原文:

Laravel is a free, open-source PHP web framework used for web application development. It was created by Taylor Otwell in 2011 and has since become one of the most popular PHP frameworks, with a large and active community of developers. Laravel is known for its elegant syntax, extensive documentation, and robust set of features, making it a popular choice for developers building modern web applications.

Watch Which PHP Framework Should You Use in 2023? on YouTube.

In this article, we will explore the key features and benefits of Laravel development, as well as how it compares to other popular PHP frameworks. We will also delve into the basics of Laravel development, including installation, routing, and working with controllers and views.

Features and Benefits of Laravel

One of the main reasons developers choose Laravel is its rich set of features and tools that make it easy to build modern, feature-rich web applications. Some of the key features and benefits of Laravel include:

MVC architecture: Laravel follows the model-view-controller (MVC) architectural pattern, which separates an application into three components: the model, which represents the data and business logic; the view, which represents the user interface; and the controller, which handles user input and communicates with the model and view. This architecture helps to ensure a clean, modular codebase and makes it easier for developers to write and maintain code.

Eloquent ORM: Laravel comes with a built-in object-relational mapper (ORM) called Eloquent, which allows developers to work with databases using simple PHP objects. This makes it easier to query and manipulate data, and helps to reduce the amount of SQL code that needs to be written.

Artisan console: Laravel includes a command-line interface called Artisan, which provides a number of useful commands for tasks such as migrating and seeding databases, creating controllers and models, and more. Artisan can also be extended with custom commands, making it easy to automate repetitive tasks and build custom workflows.

Blade template engine: Laravel uses the Blade template engine to generate HTML views, making it easy to create dynamic and reusable templates. Blade provides a number of helpful features, such as conditional statements, loops, and template inheritance, which can help to simplify view code and reduce the amount of duplication.

Homestead: Laravel includes Homestead, a pre-packaged Vagrant box that provides a convenient development environment for Laravel applications. Homestead includes a Linux-based virtual machine with PHP, MySQL, and other tools pre-installed, making it easy to get up and running with Laravel development.

Dependency injection: Laravel uses dependency injection to manage class dependencies, making it easier to manage and test code. Dependency injection allows developers to specify the dependencies of a class in the constructor, making it easier to swap out different implementations and mock dependencies for testing.

Reverse routing: Laravel's reverse routing feature allows developers to use named routes to generate URLs, making it easier to refactor routes and maintain a consistent URL structure across an application.

Package ecosystem: Laravel has a large and active community of developers who have created a wide range of packages and tools that can be easily integrated into Laravel applications. These packages can help to add new features and functionality to an application, such as payment processing, social media integration, and more.

Comparing Laravel to Other PHP Frameworks

Laravel is not the only popular PHP framework available, and developers may consider other options when choosing a framework for a new project. Some of the other popular PHP frameworks include:

CodeIgniter: CodeIgniter is a lightweight, fast and widely used. It has been around for a long time so it has the longevity and support you need for your project. CodeIgniter is extremely versatile and is almost was popular was Laravel.

Symfony: Symfony is a set of reusable PHP components that can be used to build web applications. It is known for its modular design and flexibility, and is often used as the foundation for large, complex projects. Symfony is generally considered to be more flexible and configurable than Laravel, but may require more setup and configuration.

Zend Framework: Zend Framework is a collection of PHP libraries and components that can be used to build web applications. It is known for its focus on security and performance, and is often used in enterprise-level projects. Zend Framework may be more suitable for developers who need a high level of control and customization, but may require a steeper learning curve than Laravel.

Yii: Yii is a high-performance PHP framework that is well-suited for building web applications with complex requirements. It is known for its fast performance and efficient database access, and is often used in large-scale projects. Yii may be a good choice for developers who need to optimize the performance of their application, but may be less beginner-friendly than Laravel.

Getting Started with Laravel Development

To get started with Laravel development, you will need to have PHP and a web server (such as Apache or Nginx) installed on your machine. Laravel also requires a database management system, such as MySQL or PostgreSQL.

Once these prerequisites are installed, you can use the Laravel installer tool to create a new Laravel project. This can be done by running the following command:

composer create-project --prefer-dist laravel/laravel your-project-name

This will create a new Laravel project in a directory with the specified name. You can then navigate to the project directory and start the Laravel development server by running the following command:

This will start the development server and make your Laravel application accessible at localhost:8000.

Routing in Laravel

In Laravel, routes are used to define the URLs that map to different parts of your application. Routes can be defined in the routes/web.php file, which is located in the root of your Laravel project.

Here is an example of a simple route that defines a URL and a corresponding controller action:

Route::get('/', 'HomeController@index');

In this example, the get method specifies that this route should be accessible via an HTTP GET request, and the / URL maps to the index action of the HomeController controller. When a user navigates to the / URL, the index action will be executed and the corresponding view will be displayed.

Laravel also supports route parameters, which can be used to pass data to a controller action. For example:

Route::get('users/{id}', 'UserController@show');

In this example, the {id} parameter will be passed to the show action of the UserController controller, and can be used to display data for a specific user.

Controllers and Views in Laravel

In Laravel, controllers are used to handle user input and communicate with the model and view layers of an application. Controllers are typically defined in the app/Http/Controllers directory, and are usually organized by the feature or resource they are responsible for.

Here is an example of a simple controller that defines an index action:

<?php namespace App\Http\Controllers; use Illuminate\Http\Request; class HomeController extends Controller
{ public function index() { return view('home.index'); }
}

In this example, the index action returns a view called home.index, which is located in the resources/views directory.

Views are used to define the user interface of an application and are typically written in HTML, with embedded PHP code used to display data and logic. Laravel uses the Blade template engine to make it easy to create dynamic and reusable views.

Here is an example of a simple Blade template that displays a list of users:

@extends('layout.app') @section('content') <h1>Users</h1> <ul> @foreach ($users as $user) <li>{{ $user->name }}</li> @endforeach </ul>
@endsection

In this example, the @extends directive specifies that this view should extend a layout view called layout.app, which defines the overall structure of the page. The @section directive defines a content block that can be overridden by child views, and the @foreach loop iterates over a list of users and displays their names.

Conclusion

Laravel is a powerful and popular PHP framework that makes it easy to build modern, feature-rich web applications. Its rich set of features and tools, including MVC architecture, Eloquent ORM, Artisan console, Blade template engine, and dependency injection, make it a popular choice for developers building all kinds of web applications. Whether you are a beginner or an experienced developer, Laravel offers a friendly and powerful development environment that can help you build high-quality web applications quickly and efficiently.

View More On YouTube

Check out my video where I give you the pros and cons of popular PHP frameworks you should use in 2023. You can see the embedded video below or click the link at the beginning of the article to watch it on YouTube.
item.author: thedevdrawer

item.title: Rust 中的函数和控制流 | Functions and Control Flow in Rust
item.description:
通过探索函数和控制流的基础知识来继续您的 Rust 之旅。

首先，让我们回顾一下。 它以其安全性、速度和并发性而闻名，使其成为构建高性能系统的热门选择，这就是我们对其进行更多了解的原因。

控制流

控制流是指语句、表达式和其他语言结构在程序中的执行顺序。 在 Rust 的编程语言中，控制流是使用关键字、表达式和语句的组合来实现的。

这允许程序员指定执行代码块的条件。 例如：

 if x > 5 { // code to be executed if x is greater than 5 }

它允许程序员将一个值与多个模式进行比较，并为每个匹配项执行不同的代码。 例如：

 let x = 5; match x { 1 => { // code to be executed if x is equal to 1 } 2 | 3 | 4 => { // code to be executed if x is equal to 2, 3, or 4 } _ => { // code to be executed if x does not match any of the above patterns } }

这些对于重复某个代码块直到满足某个条件很有用。 例如：

 loop { // code to be executed repeatedly if x > 5 { break; // breaks out of the loop if x is greater than 5 } }

除了这些控制流结构之外，Rust 还提供了其他几个关键字和表达式来控制程序的流，包括 while、for 和 continue。

在 Rust 中，while 关键字用于创建 while 循环。 它重复执行一段代码，直到满足指定的条件。 它重复执行一段代码给定的次数。 这可用于根据某些条件跳过循环的某些迭代。 使用 continue 的语法是：

 while condition { if some_condition { continue; } // code to execute }

在此示例中，如果 some_condition 为真，则在 while 循环中使用 continue 关键字来跳过循环的当前迭代。 只有当 some_condition 为 false 时，循环内的代码才会执行​​。

这些特性共同为 Rust 程序员提供了一套强大的工具来实现他们程序的控制流。

职能

函数是任何编程语言的基本构建块，Rust 也不例外。 在 Rust 中，函数是使用fn关键字创建的，后跟函数名称和一组包含函数参数的括号。 例如：

 fn add(x: i32, y: i32) -> i32 { // code to perform addition goes here }

在上面的示例中，参数列表后的 -> i32 语法表示该函数将返回一个 i32（32 位带符号整数）值。

控制流是 Rust 中函数的一个重要方面，因为它允许程序员根据接收到的输入指定函数可以采用的不同路径。 例如，上面的 add 函数可以使用 if 语句来检查相加的两个数字是否均为正数，如果不是，则返回不同的值：

 fn add(x: i32, y: i32) -> i32 { if x > 0 && y > 0 { // code to perform addition goes here } else { return 0; // return 0 if either x or y are not positive } }

在函数中使用控制流的另一种方法是使用匹配表达式。 它们允许函数处理多种情况并根据输入返回不同的值。 例如，以下函数使用匹配表达式来确定给定数字是正数、负数还是零，并返回表示结果的字符串：

 fn classify(x: i32) -> String { match x { 0 => "zero".to_string(), n if n > 0 => "positive".to_string(), n if n < 0 => "negative".to_string(), } }

上面的代码示例定义了一个名为 classify 的函数，它接受一个 i32（32 位有符号整数）值作为其参数并返回一个字符串值。 该函数使用匹配表达式将参数 x 的值与三种不同的模式进行比较：

  1. 第一个模式 0 匹配 x 的值（如果它等于零）。 在这种情况下，代码“zero”.to_string() 被执行，它创建一个包含文本“zero”的新字符串并将其作为函数的结果返回。

  2. 第二个模式，n if n > 0，匹配 x 的值，如果它大于零。 此模式还将 x 的值绑定到名为 n 的变量，如果此模式匹配，则可在执行的代码中使用该变量。 在这种情况下，执行代码“positive”.to_string()，它创建一个包含文本“positive”的新字符串并将其作为函数的结果返回。

  3. 第三个模式，n if n < 0，如果 x 小于零，则匹配 x 的值。 此模式还将 x 的值绑定到变量 n，如果此模式匹配，则可在执行的代码中使用该变量。 在这种情况下，代码“negative”.to_string() 被执行，它创建一个包含文本“negative”的新字符串并将其作为函数的结果返回。

如果 x 的值不匹配这些模式中的任何一个，匹配表达式将返回错误。 发生这种情况是因为匹配表达式是详尽无遗的，也就是说，它必须涵盖所有可能的情况。 然而，在这个例子中，除了模式涵盖的三种情况之外，x 不可能取任何值，所以函数是正确的。

这段代码是一个很好的例子，说明了如何在 Rust 函数中使用控制流来处理情况并根据输入返回值。

既然我们已经发布了 Rust 基础知识系列文章，您应该了解这种编程语言的基础知识。 现在您可以开始使用 Rust 语言提供的功能和工具构建简单的应用程序。

亲自动手

总结我们在前三篇文章中学到的知识，让我们构建一个函数来检查一个数字是否是 Rust 中斐波那契数列的一部分：

 fn is_fibonacci(n: u64) -> bool { let mut a = 0; let mut b = 1; while b < n { let c = a + b; a = b; b = c; } return b == n; }

该函数使用一个简单的循环来生成斐波那契数列。 它从序列中的前两个数字 0 和 1 开始，然后使用 while 循环通过将前两个数字相加来生成序列中的下一个数字。 循环继续，直到生成的数字大于或等于 n。 如果生成的数字等于 n，则函数返回 true，因为 n 是斐波那契数列的一部分。 如果生成的数字不等于 n，则函数返回 false，因为 n 不是斐波那契数列的一部分。

结论

总之，Rust 是一种强大而高效的编程语言，具有丰富的功能和工具集，可用于构建广泛的应用程序。 通过这些文章中提供的信息，我们的社区应该做好充分准备来试验 Rust 并了解更多关于它的功能。

发表评论，让我们知道您想接下来学习什么。 您是否希望我们在以后的文章中涵盖 Rust 的特定主题或方面？ 您对 Rust 有任何问题或疑虑希望我们解决吗？ 我们为您打造了这个空间，因此我们很乐意听取您的反馈和建议。

关于 Vaultree

借助 Vaultree，组织可以大规模处理、搜索和计算无处不在的数据，而无需交出加密密钥或解密服务器端。 如果发生泄漏，Vaultree 的使用中数据加密会持续存在，使数据无法被坏人使用。 将 Vaultree 无缝集成到现有数据库技术中，无需更改技术或平台。 欲了解更多信息，请访问www.vaultree.com。



原文:

Continue your Rust journey by Exploring the basics of functions and control flow.

First, let’s recap. Rust is a programming language developed by Mozilla Corporation. It is famous for its safety, speed, and concurrency, making it a popular choice for building high-performance systems, and that’s why we are learning more about it.

Control Flow

Control flow refers to the order in which statements, expressions, and other language constructs are executed in a program. In Rust’s programming language, control flow is implemented using a combination of keywords, expressions, and statements.

One of the fundamental control flow constructs in Rust is the if statement. That allows a programmer to specify the condition for executing a block of code. For example:

if x > 5 { 
// code to be executed if x is greater than 5 
}

The if statement can also be combined with an else clause to specify code to be executed if the condition is not met:

if x > 5 { 
// code to be executed if x is greater than 5 
} else { 
// code to be executed if x is not greater than 5 
}

Another important control flow construct in Rust is the match expression. It allows a programmer to compare a value against multiple patterns and execute different codes for each match. For example:

let x = 5; match x {
1 => { 
// code to be executed if x is equal to 1 
} 
2 | 3 | 4 => { 
// code to be executed if x is equal to 2, 3, or 4 
}
 _ => {
 // code to be executed if x does not match any of the above patterns 
} }

Rust also provides the loop keyword, allowing the creation of infinite loops. These can be useful for repeating a certain block of code until a certain condition is met. For example:

loop {
// code to be executed repeatedly 
if x > 5 { 
break; // breaks out of the loop if x is greater than 5
} }

In addition to these control flow constructs, Rust provides several other keywords and expressions to control the flow of a program, including while, for, and continue.

In Rust, the while keyword is used to create a while loop. It repeatedly executes a block of code until a specified condition is met. The syntax for a while loop is:

while condition {
// code to execute
}

The for keyword is used to create a for loop. It repeatedly executes a block of code for a given number of times. The syntax for a for loop is:

for variable in range {
 // code to execute 
}

The continue keyword is used within a loop to immediately skip the rest of the current iteration of the loop and start the next iteration. This can be used to skip over certain iterations of the loop based on certain conditions. The syntax for using continue is:

while condition {
 if some_condition {
 continue; 
} // code to execute 
}

In this example, the continue keyword is used within a while loop to skip over the current iteration of the loop if some_condition is true. The code inside the loop will only be executed if some_condition is false.

Together, these features provide Rust programmers with a powerful set of tools for implementing the control flow of their programs.

Functions

Functions are a fundamental building block of any programming language, and Rust is no exception. In Rust, functions are created using the fn keyword, followed by the name of the function and a set of parentheses containing the function’s parameters. For example:

fn add(x: i32, y: i32) -> i32 {
 // code to perform addition goes here 
}

Functions in Rust can also return a value using the return keyword. In the example above, the -> i32 syntax after the parameter list indicates that the function will return an i32 (32-bit signed integer) value.

Control flow is an important aspect of functions in Rust, as it allows a programmer to specify the different paths a function can take depending on the input it receives. For example, the add function above could use an if statement to check whether the two numbers being added are both positive, and return a different value if that is not the case:

fn add(x: i32, y: i32) -> i32 {
 if x > 0 && y > 0 {
 // code to perform addition goes here 
} else {
return 0; // return 0 if either x or y are not positive
 } }

Another way control flow can be used in functions is by using match expressions. They allow a function to handle multiple cases and return different values depending on the input. For example, the following function uses a match expression to determine whether a given number is positive, negative, or zero, and returns a string indicating the result:

fn classify(x: i32) -> String { 
match x {
0 => "zero".to_string(), 
n if n > 0 => "positive".to_string(),
n if n < 0 => "negative".to_string(),
} }

The code example above defines a function named classify that takes an i32 (32-bit signed integer) value as its parameter and returns a String value. The function uses a match expression to compare the value of the parameter x against three different patterns:

  1. The first pattern, 0, matches the value of x if it equals to zero. In this case, the code “zero”.to_string() is executed, which creates a new string containing the text “zero” and returns it as the result of the function.

  2. The second pattern, n if n > 0, matches the value of x if it is greater than zero. This pattern also binds the value of x to a variable named n, which can be used in the code that is executed if this pattern matches. In this case, the code “positive”.to_string() is executed, which creates a new string containing the text “positive” and returns it as the result of the function.

  3. The third pattern, n if n < 0, matches the value of x if it is less than zero. This pattern also binds the value of x to the variable n, which can be used in the code that is executed if this pattern matches. In this case, the code “negative”.to_string() is executed, which creates a new string containing the text “negative” and returns it as the result of the function.

If the value of x does not match any of these patterns, the match expression will return an error. This happens because the match expression is exhaustive, that is, it must cover all possible cases. In this example, however, it is not possible for x to take on any value other than the three cases covered by the patterns, so the function is correct as written.

This code is a great example of how control flow can be used in a Rust function to handle cases and return values depending on the input.

Now that we have published this series of articles on Rust fundamentals, you should understand the basics of this programming language. Now you can start building simple applications in Rust, using the features and tools the language provides.

Hands-on

Wrapping up what we’ve learnt in the last three articles and let’s build a function that checks if a number is part of the Fibonacci sequence in Rust:

fn is_fibonacci(n: u64) -> bool {
 let mut a = 0; 
let mut b = 1; 
while b < n {
 let c = a + b;
 a = b; 
b = c; 
} 
return b == n; 
}

This function uses a simple loop to generate the Fibonacci sequence. It starts with the first two numbers in the sequence, 0 and 1, and then uses a while loop to generate the next numbers in the sequence by adding the previous two numbers together. The loop continues until the generated number is greater than or equal to n. If the generated number is equal to n, the function returns true because n is part of the Fibonacci sequence. If the generated number is not equal to n, the function returns false because n is not part of the Fibonacci sequence.

Conclusion

In conclusion, Rust is a powerful and efficient programming language with a rich set of features and tools for building a wide range of applications. With the information provided in these articles, our community should be well-equipped to experiment with Rust and learn more about its capabilities.

Leave a comment and let us know what you want to learn next. Is there a particular topic or aspect of Rust that you would like us to cover in future articles? Do you have any questions or concerns about Rust you would like us to address? We built this space for you, so we’d love to hear your feedback and suggestions.

About Vaultree

Vaultree has developed the world’s first Fully Functional Data-in-Use Encryption solution that solves the industry’s fundamental security issue: persistent data encryption, even in the event of a leak. Vaultree enables enterprises, including those in the financial services and healthcare / pharmaceutical sectors, to mitigate the great financial, cyber, legal, and business risk of a data breach in plain text. With Vaultree, organisations process, search, and compute ubiquitous data at scale, without ever having to surrender encryption keys or decrypt server-side. If a leak occurs, Vaultree’s data-in-use encryption persists, rendering the data unusable to bad actors. Integrating Vaultree into existing database technologies is seamless, requiring no technology or platform changes. Vaultree is a privately held company based in Ireland and the U.S.

For more information, please visit www.vaultree.com.
item.author: opedroaravena

item.title: 联系招聘人员 | Reaching Out To Recruiters
item.description:
如果说我从销售中学到了一件事，那就是坚持不懈。 每一个否定都会让你更接近肯定。 这在寻找新工作时尤其适用。

在我撰写本文时，我正处于从销售职业转型的过程中，目前正在申请软件工程职位。 我仍然使用传统方法（LinkedIn 和 Indeed）申请尽可能多的工作，但没有什么比接触并获得可能的推荐或直接联系招聘人员具有更高的投资回报率。

下面的消息是你在发送连接请求时可以添加的注释，由于你只有 300 个字符，所以你必须简明扼要。 一旦他们将您添加回来，您就有了联系方式，您可以每隔几个月与他们联系一次。

嗨 [姓名]，看到您正在 [公司] 招聘 [职位]。 我有[relevant experience matching job posting]的经验，非常适合。 愿意讨论！

你可以说任何话，但像这样的方法对于同一家公司的多个招聘人员来说是最直接和可复制的。

如果您有任何其他遵循的模板，我很乐意在评论中阅读它们。

永恒的开发帖子...

我多年前就希望知道的 Git 概念

开发人员使用最多的技术不是 Javascript。

它不是 Python 或 HTML。

它甚至很少在面试中被提及或被列为工作的先决条件。

当然，我说的是 Git 和版本控制。

穆罕默德·萨勒曼 - 22 年 12 月 3 日

托马斯·费尔南德斯 - 22 年 11 月 24 日

一旦暂停，dessygil 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，dessygil 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，dessygil 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 dessygil 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您保护 DEV 社区 👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 您可以通过以下方式标记 dessygil：

降低 dessygil 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 dessygil 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

If there is one thing I've learned from sales, it's to persevere. Each no brings you closer to a yes. This especially applies when looking for a new job.

As I write this, I'm in the middle of a career transition from sales and am currently applying for software engineering jobs. I still apply to as many jobs as possible using the traditional methods (LinkedIn and Indeed), but nothing has a higher ROI than reaching out and getting a possible referral or contacting the recruiters directly.

The message below is a note you can add when you send a connection request, and since you only have 300 characters, you have to be concise. Once they add you back, you have a line of contact, and you can reach out to them every few months.

Hi [name], saw you were hiring for [role] at [company]. I have experience in [relevant experience matching job posting], which would be a great fit. Would love to discuss!

You can say anything, but a method like this is the most direct and reproducible for multiple recruiters at the same company.

If you have any other templates you follow, I would love to read them in the comments.

Timeless DEV post...

Git Concepts I Wish I Knew Years Ago

The most used technology by developers is not Javascript.

It's not Python or HTML.

It hardly even gets mentioned in interviews or listed as a pre-requisite for jobs.

I'm talking about Git and version control of course.

Ebenezer Enietan (Niza) - Dec 3 '22

Muhammad Salman - Dec 3 '22

Tomas Fernandez - Nov 24 '22

Once suspended, dessygil will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, dessygil will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by dessygil will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If dessygil is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging dessygil will restore default visibility to their posts.

item.title: 我如何开设一个 coinbase 商业账户 | How do i open a coinbase business account
item.description:
安迪·赫森

发表于 1 月 4 日

使用 Coinbase 商业帐户，您可以安全地买卖加密货币、管理多个用户和钱包，以及查看有关您的活动的详细报告。 要创建 Coinbase 商业账户，您需要提供一些关于您的企业的额外信息，例如您的企业名称、地址和联系信息。 您可能还需要通过提供营业执照或公司章程等文件来验证您的企业身份。

要开设 Coinbase 商业账户，请按照下列步骤操作：

  1. 转到 Coinbase 网站并单击“注册”按钮。
  2. 选择“商业”作为帐户类型。
  3. 输入您的公司名称、电子邮件地址并创建密码。
  4. 单击“创建帐户”按钮。
  5. 按照提示完成注册过程。 这可能包括提供有关您的企业的其他信息，例如您的企业地址和联系信息，以及验证您的企业身份。

完成注册过程后，您可以使用您的 Coinbase 商业帐户买卖加密货币、管理多个用户和钱包，以及查看有关您的活动的详细报告。

一旦暂停，hearsonandy 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

如果 hearsonandy 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

报告其他不当行为

不屈不挠的 hearsonandy 将恢复他们帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Andy Hearson

Posted on Jan 4

Coinbase offers a Coinbase Business account that is designed specifically for businesses. With a Coinbase Business account, you can securely buy and sell cryptocurrencies, manage multiple users and wallets, and view detailed reports on your activity. Coinbase Business accounts also have access to additional features, such as recurring buys and higher limits. To create a Coinbase Business account, you will need to provide some additional information about your business, such as your business name, address, and contact information. You may also need to verify your business's identity by providing documentation such as a business license or articles of incorporation.

To open a Coinbase Business account, follow these steps:

  1. Go to the Coinbase website and click on the "Sign Up" button.
  2. Select "Business" as the account type.
  3. Enter your business name, email address, and create a password.
  4. Click on the "Create Account" button.
  5. Follow the prompts to complete the registration process. This may include providing additional information about your business, such as your business address and contact information, as well as verifying your business's identity.

Once you have completed the registration process, you can use your Coinbase Business account to buy and sell cryptocurrencies, manage multiple users and wallets, and view detailed reports on your activity.

Adamo Software - Jan 5 '21

InterSystems Developer - Nov 11 '20

Franco Scarpa - Nov 17 '20
